channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: FBI director, Homeland Security secretary refuse to publicly testify, Senate chairman says
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray and DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas after facing backlash for skipping public national security hearings this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic chair blasts Mayorkas, Wray for failure to testify; threats hearing postponed
item.description: Gary Peters criticized DHS head Alejandro Mayorkas and FBI head Christopher Wray for failing to appear for a hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith: Trump may have ulterior motive for Gaetz pick
item.description: Stephen A. Smith says that “under normal circumstances” Matt Gaetz would never be confirmed as the nation's new attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate rejects effort from Bernie Sanders to block some weapons for Israel over Gaza deaths
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Wednesday rejected attempts by Sen. Bernie Sanders Wednesday to block sales of offensive weapons to Israel for its war in Gaza over mounting civilian deaths there.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Biden has become notably quiet after the 2024 election and Democrats' loss
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has been notably quiet since the Democrats' gut-wrenching defeat at the polls.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA head sounds the alarm over disaster funding after double hurricanes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The money available to help communities hit by disasters has shrunk after back-to-back hurricanes Helene and Milton, and the funding problems might jeopardize the government's ability to respond to new emergencies in the future, the Federal Emergency Management Agency chief said We
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Trump has named his former acting attorney general, Matthew Whitaker, as the next ambassador to NATO, selecting him to fill the role at a crucial time amid Russia's war in Ukraine and tensions over defense spending across the Western security alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Matthew Whitaker, Trump’s pick to be America's ambassador to NATO
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has tapped former acting attorney general Matthew Whitaker as U.S. ambassador to NATO — set to make him the nation's representative to a bedrock Western alliance that the president-elect has repeatedly relished criticizing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaetz meets with Senate Republicans as House ethics panel considers releasing report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz, Trump's nominee to serve as Attorney General, faces an uphill battle as the House ethics panel considers releasing report on allegations involving sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, MPR News

item.title: Wrangling over whether to punt government funding to Trump heats up
item.description: The debate among Republicans over whether to punt government funding into the new year is heating up, as lawmakers race toward their next shutdown deadline. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) this week said lawmakers are running out of time until the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's immigration grand strategy casts wide net
item.description: President-elect Trump is returning to the White House looking to dwarf his first term's significant impact on immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks business executive Linda McMahon to lead the Education Department
item.description: McMahon is a professional wrestling business magnate and co-chair of President-elect Donald Trump's transition team. She has limited experience working with K-12 public schools.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Trump to nominate Mehmet Oz for CMS administrator
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he is nominating television personality Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) administrator. Oz unsuccessfully ran for Senate in Pennsylvania in 2022 with Trump's endorsement and has been a Trump ally ever since.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries wins reelection as House Democratic leader
item.description: House Leader Hakeem Jeffries won reelection Tuesday as the Democratic leader, receiving support from his colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Defense pick Peter Hegseth paid accuser to protect his career, his lawyer confirms
item.description: The Fox News host reached a settlement to resolve an accusation against him, but a recently released memo brought the issue back into the public eye.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Trump taps Sean Duffy, Fox host and former congressman, for transportation secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Sean Duffy, a Fox Business host and former Republican congressman from Wisconsin, to head the Department of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Biden asks Congress for about $100 billion in disaster relief funding
item.description: The largest chunk of funding — about $40 billion — would be for FEMA's disaster relief fund, so that it has enough money to last through the coming year. But there are requests for 16 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Trump taps FCC's Brendan Carr to lead the agency
item.description: Carr was seen as a pretty conventional Republican with a pro-corporate outlook for most of his career. More recently, he has embraced Trumpian themes about social media, tech and television companies.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg called to apologize for saying bakery denied order over politics
item.description: New York City politicians are calling on Whoopi Goldberg to apologize for suggesting on air that a local bakery had declined to make “The View” co-host a batch of desserts for her birthday because of her political beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden and Xi take a first step to limit AI and nuclear decisions at their last meeting
item.description: The leaders agreed that humans should control decisions to use nuclear weapons — not AI. And they talked about the importance of stability during the transition to a new U.S. administration.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s criminal defense attorneys offered DOJ roles
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Thursday he will nominate three of his personal criminal defense attorneys to serve in senior Justice Department roles. Todd Blanche will serve as deputy attorney general, and Emil Bove will serve as principal associate deputy attorney general, the department's No.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Controversial bathroom bill clears Ohio Senate, awaits Gov. DeWine's signature
item.description: The Ohio Senate voted 24-7 in favor of the ban, sending the measure to DeWine's desk for signature before it can become law.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, Fox 5
